---
title: "GTF edition"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-04"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

```{r}
#Install important packages
install.packages("readxl")
install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages("fs")
install.packages("rlist")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("tibble")
install.packages("data.table", dependencies=TRUE)
install.packages("rapportools")
```

```{r}
#Launch important packages
library(rtracklayer)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(rlist)
library(data.table)
library(rapportools)
```

```{r}
#Import the gtf file as a data frame with all columns better than the Kwok method
directory_in = "/Users/yahya/Desktop/STAR-aligner/GTF-FASTA/"
setwd(directory_in)

g <- readGFF("Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.115.gtf")
```

```{r}
data.table_gtf_protein_coding = g %>%
  dplyr::filter(gene_biotype == "protein_coding" ) %>% #keep protein-coding genes only
  dplyr::filter(type == "transcript") %>% # keep transcript genes only
  dplyr::filter(seqid != "MT") #Remove mitochondrial genes (to discuss?)
```

```{r}
directory_output = "/Users/yahya/Desktop/STAR-aligner/GTF-FASTA/"
setwd(directory_output)

filename_output = "GTF_Protein_Coding_Genes"
write.table(data.table_gtf_protein_coding, filename_output, col.names = T, row.names = F, sep = "\t", quote = F)
```
